task:
Implement the CodeRabbit Coding Plan for issue #2573: Brain Dump to Outline — fix CR feedback and re-promote ## Instructions 1. `cd ~/2025slideheroes-sophie` 2. `git fetch upstream && git push origin upstream/dev:dev --force` 3. `git checkout upstream/dev && git checkout -b sophie/issue-2573` 4. Read the full issue: `gh issue view 2573 --repo slideheroes/2025slideheroes` 5. Implement according to the Coding Plan below 6. Run `pnpm format:fix && pnpm lint:fix && pnpm typecheck` before committing 7. Commit with: `git commit -m "feat: Brain Dump to Outline — fix CR feedback and re-promote (#2573)"` 8. Push: `git push origin sophie/issue-2573` 9. Open PR on the **fork** for CodeRabbit review (NOT on upstream): ```bash gh pr create --repo slideheroes/2025slideheroes-sophie \ --base dev \ --head sophie/issue-2573 \ --title "feat: Brain Dump to Outline — fix CR feedback and re-promote" \ --body "Implements slideheroes/2025slideheroes#2573 Implemented per CodeRabbit Coding Plan. > This PR is on the fork for CodeRabbit review. It will be promoted to upstream automatically once CR is satisfied and CI passes." ``` **IMPORTANT:** - Open the PR on the FORK (`slideheroes/2025slideheroes-sophie`), NOT on upstream (`slideheroes/2025slideheroes`). - The PR will be automatically promoted to upstream after CodeRabbit review. - Do NOT open a PR on upstream directly. ## CodeRabbit Coding Plan ## Coding Plan ### Summary - Leverage existing canonical enum schemas (`PresentationTypeSchema`, `QuestionTypeSchema`) instead of creating new validation — they already exist and match UI options - Add defensive parsing for LLM responses: explicit prompt instructions, fence-stripping, and Zod validation - Fix the argument_map access to align with the actual `ArgumentMapNode` tree structure that's stored in Supabase - Single-line config fix for the routing bug ## Implementation Steps ### Phase 1: Schema & Validation Hardening Addresses validation gaps — missing input bounds, no LLM response validation, and missing barrel exports. All changes are in schema files or adding new schema definitions.
Task 1: Create Barrel Export for Blocks Schemas The `blocks/_lib/schemas/` directory has no `index.ts`, requiring full-path imports. - Create `apps/web/app/home/(user)/ai/blocks/_lib/schemas/index.ts` - Re-export `SubmitBuildingBlocksSchema` and type `SubmitBuildingBlocksData` - Update the import in `blocks/_actions/submitBuildingBlocksAction.ts` to use the barrel
Task 2: Fix Input Bounds with Enum Validation `presentation_type` and `question_type` are `z.string().min(1)` but canonical enums already exist and should be used. - In `blocks/_lib/schemas/submit-building-blocks.schema.ts`, import `PresentationTypeSchema` and `QuestionTypeSchema` from `../../_lib/schemas/presentation-artifacts` - Replace `z.string().min(1)` definitions with the imported enum schemas - This enforces the 4 valid presentation types and 6 valid question types at the schema level
Task 3: Add LLM Response Validation Schema The RAG path parses LLM JSON response with no structural validation. - In `_lib/schemas/presentation-artifacts.ts` (or a new file in the same directory), add a Zod schema for the LLM outline response: ``` OutlineSectionSchema: z.object({ title: z.string(), content: z.string() }) LLMOutlineResponseSchema: z.object({ sections: z.array(OutlineSectionSchema) }) ``` - Export both the schema and the inferred type - Update the schemas barrel `_lib/schemas/index.ts` to include the new exports
🤖 Prompt for AI agents ``` This phase hardens validation by wiring up existing canonical enums and adding a new Zod schema for LLM responses — no runtime logic changes yet. **Task 1: Create barrel export for blocks schemas** - Create `apps/web/app/home/(user)/ai/blocks/_lib/schemas/index.ts` - Re-export `SubmitBuildingBlocksSchema` and the `SubmitBuildingBlocksData` type from the existing schema file - Update the import in `blocks/_actions/submitBuildingBlocksAction.ts` to point to the barrel instead of the full path **Task 2: Replace loose string validation with canonical enum schemas** - In `blocks/_lib/schemas/submit-building-blocks.schema.ts`, import `PresentationTypeSchema` and `QuestionTypeSchema` from `../../_lib/schemas/presentation-artifacts` - Replace the `z.string().min(1)` usages for `presentation_type` and `question_type` with these imported enum schemas — this enforces only the 4 valid presentation types and 6 valid question types at the Zod boundary **Task 3: Add Zod schema for LLM outline response** - In `_lib/schemas/presentation-artifacts.ts` (or a new file in the same directory), define and export: - `OutlineSectionSchema`: `z.object({ title: z.string(), content: z.string() })` - `LLMOutlineResponseSchema`: `z.object({ sections: z.array(OutlineSectionSchema) })` - The inferred TypeScript types for both - Add these new exports to the schemas barrel at `_lib/schemas/index.ts` ```
### Phase 2: Runtime Logic Fixes Fixes the actual runtime bugs — JSON parsing failures, incorrect data access, and broken routing.
Task 1: Fix JSON Prompt and Add Fence Stripping The RAG prompt says "Return ONLY valid JSON" but LLMs often wrap in markdown fences, causing parse failures. - In `outline/_actions/generate-outline.action.ts`, update the system prompt in `generateOutlineWithRAG()` to explicitly instruct: "Do not wrap in markdown code fences" - Before the `JSON.parse(content)` call, add a preprocessing step to strip ` ```json ` and ` ``` ` delimiters if present - A simple regex like `/^```(?:json)?\n?([\s\S]*?)\n?```$/` or checking for leading/trailing fence markers will handle this
Task 2: Apply Zod Validation After JSON Parse After stripping fences, the parsed JSON still needs structural validation. - Import `LLMOutlineResponseSchema` from the schemas - Replace the loose `let parsed: { sections?: ... }` with a proper Zod `safeParse` - On validation failure, log the raw response shape for debugging and throw a descriptive error - Use `validated.sections` downstream instead of `parsed.sections ?? []`
Task 3: Fix Argument Map Key Access The non-RAG path reads `main_argument`, `answer`, `supporting_points` but the actual `ArgumentMapNode` structure uses `text`, `type`, `id`, `children`. - In `generate-outline.action.ts`, locate the non-RAG argument_map processing block (around lines 236-268) - Import `ArgumentMapNodeSchema` from `_lib/schemas/presentation-artifacts` - Parse `assembleOutput.argument_map` with the schema (use `safeParse` to handle malformed data gracefully) - Replace the key access logic: - Main argument comes from `root.text` (the top-level claim) - Supporting points come from `root.children.map(child => child.text)` for direct children - Add a warning log if parsing fails or the tree is empty, so silent failures become visible
Task 4: Fix Storyboard Path Missing Slash In `paths.config.ts`, the storyboard path is `"home/ai/storyboard"` ## Coding Rules # Coding Rules These rules document the actual code quality patterns used in the SlideHeroes codebase. ## TypeScript Patterns - Strict mode compliance: enable all strict TypeScript options - Use explicit local type aliases rather than inline complex types - Use `z.infer` for Zod-derived types - Use `instanceof Error` narrowing in catch blocks instead of `instanceof` checks on non-Error values - Never use `any` - use `unknown` with type guards when needed ## API Route Patterns - Use Next.js 15 style with `params` as Promise - Use named exports (GET, POST, PATCH, DELETE) for route handlers - Use `.catch(() => ({}))` for body parsing error handling - Use `NextResponse.json({ error })` response shape - Validate input at boundaries using Zod schemas ## Prisma Patterns - Import Prisma client from `@/lib/db` - Use conditional spread for PATCH updates (e.g., `...(data || {})`) - Use `.catch(() => null)` for not-found handling ## Import Conventions - Use `@/` alias for src imports (e.g., `@/components/Button`) - Import from specific files, avoid barrel exports - Separate client and server imports - never mix them from the same file ## Error Handling - Use tight try/catch around specific I/O operations only - Use `logger.error({ err }, '...')` for structured logging - Import logger from `@kit/shared/logger` - Handle errors gracefully using try/catch and appropriate error types - Use `isRedirectError` from `next/dist/client/components/redirect-error` for client-side redirect handling ## React Patterns - Encapsulate repeated blocks of code into reusable local components - Write small, composable, explicit, well-named components - Always use `react-hook-form` and `@kit/ui/form` for forms - Use client components only when needed for interactivity (use `use client` directive) - Add `data-testid` for E2E tests where appropriate - Prefer server-side data fetching using RSC - Display loading indicators where appropriate ## Next.js Patterns - Use `enhanceAction` for Server Actions from `@kit/next/actions` - Use `use server` in server action files - Export page components using the `withI18n` utility - Add well-written page metadata to pages - Redirect using `redirect` following a server action instead of using client-side router ## Data Fetching Patterns - Use async server components that call loader functions for initial data fetching - Use `enhanceAction` for create/update/delete operations with schema validation - Use `Promise.all()` for parallel data fetching in Server Components ## Linting and Formatting Before committing, always run: ```bash pnpm format:fix && pnpm lint:fix && pnpm typecheck ``` ## Component Organization - Route-specific components: `_components/` directories - Route utilities: `_lib/` for client, `_lib/server/` for server-side - Global components: Root-level directories ## Naming Conventions - Pages: `page.tsx` (Next.js convention) - Loaders: `{feature}-page.loader.ts` - Actions: `{feature}-server-actions.ts` - Schemas: `{feature}.schema.ts` - Components: `kebab-case.tsx`
label: neo-issue-2573
